evidence_quotes.quote: updated version was in essence an entirely new game, thus deleting my ability to continue using what I had purchased.
digital ownership of purchases that should have no limit of ownership as they are a product, not a service or currency.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I would like to add my request that this act also takes into account the ownership of digital products like video games. This request is made because of examples like Warcraft 3 and Overwatch, both games I adored and bought that were deleted by the producer when they created what they called an updated version of it. And while similar, this updated version was in essence an entirely new game, thus deleting my ability to continue using what I had purchased. There are many, many more examples that could be mentioned and many of those I see in other people's feedback. This idea is also backed by the Stop Killing Games citizen's initiative which has reached 1.4 million signatures, of which I'm sure over a million are valid. I heavily support this act and am grateful to live somewhere where consumer rights are protected to the level that they are, I would be happy to support it as is, but far happier to support it with the addition to protect digital ownership of purchases that should have no limit of ownership as they are a product, not a service or currency. Thank you kindly and in advance.
